laws.title: The Commerce Legislation and Labor Aggravations Reformations (CLEAR)
laws.status_basis: Listed in 'Repealed Laws', but the source is a full reorganization act with express, determinable Code amendments (5 U.S.C. 101; PL 12-80 sec.13) plus a large uncodified new-department framework. Repeal of the whole act cannot be verified from this source alone; the enactment itself is not self-repealing. Treated as partially-current: the express Code amendments and self-repeals of prior PLs are operative textual events regardless of the act's later fate.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'CLEAR'.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be referred to by its short title, “The Commerce Legislation and Labor Aggravations Reformations” or “CLEAR”.”
provisions.notes: Short title note only if act retained.

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession clause.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable ... shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately upon enactment.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: General provision incorporating PL 1-1 code applicability.
provisions.evidence: “Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable.”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of Department, Secretary, revoke, suspend, blacklist, disperse, manage, event.
provisions.evidence: “For the purposes of this act- (a) The term “Department” means the Department of Commerce and Labor...”
provisions.notes: Duplicate SEC5 numbering in source (definitions and department both labeled SEC5).

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 4-29 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal PL 4-29 entirely.
provisions.evidence: “Public Law 4-29 shall be repealed, in its entirety.”
provisions.notes: Target Code sections not identified in source.

provisions.text_summary: Establishes Department of Commerce and Labor; reallocates Labor, Commerce, SBA, USTR, Ex-Im, USTDA, PTO, Census, NLRB, FCC, FTC; leadership; structure; powers.
provisions.target: affected chapters of t15, t29, t35, t47, t51, t19
provisions.evidence: “Under the Executive Branch there shall be a department known as the Department of Commerce and Labor.”; “The United States Department of Labor (under 29 U.S. Code Chapter 12) ... are hereby allocated under the Department of Commerce and Labor.”
provisions.notes: Organic agency-creation language; transfers of functions. Ordinarily statutory/transfer notes, not new positive-law section text, absent an assigned Code home.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Section 13 of Public Law 12-80 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Strike sec.13 of PL 12-80.
provisions.evidence: “Section 13 of Public Law 12-80 shall be repealed in its entirety.”

provisions.text_summary: Substitutes new text for Section 13 of PL 12-80 (FEC/DOCAL remedies, DC district court enforcement, review, costs).
provisions.target: PL 12-80 sec.13 (Government Labor Management Act, wherever codified)
provisions.exact_change: Replace sec.13 with the enumerated (a)-(d) DOCAL remedies/enforcement text.
provisions.evidence: “Section 13 of Public Law 12-80 shall now read as; (a) Remedies of the FEC and DOCAL may include...”
provisions.notes: Determinable full replacement text present. Requires real textual amendment where PL 12-80 sec.13 is codified; if not in positive-law Code, statutory note.

provisions.text_summary: Criminal/civil penalties for operating a business or event without a license/permit.
provisions.evidence: “The operation of a business without a business licence shall be illegal, the owner(s) shall be liable for a fine of up to twenty-five thousand dollars (USD$25,000) or 2 days in federal prison.”

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 5 U.S.C. 101 and replaces it with a new list of Executive departments substituting 'Department of Commerce and Labor'.
provisions.exact_change: Strike existing 5 U.S.C. 101 and set the executive-departments list to: State; Treasury; Defense; Justice; Commerce and Labor; Homeland Security. (Note: source list omits several departments present in real 5 U.S.C. 101 — verbatim replacement as enacted.)
provisions.evidence: “5 U.S.Code § 101 shall be repealed in its entirety. 5 U.S.Code §101 shall now read as; The Executive departments are: a. The Department of State. ... e. The Department of Commerce and Labor. f. The Department of Homeland Security.”
provisions.notes: This is the placement anchor for the current note (/us/usc/t5/s101). This is a genuine express textual amendment, not a note.

current_implementation.detail: Only a boilerplate codification note was added at t5 s101. The enactment makes a genuine express amendment to 5 U.S.C. 101 (SEC8) and to PL 12-80 sec.13 (SEC6) that were NOT applied as textual amendments. Trello link present; note declares status 'Repealed or superseded' which is not established by the source.

recommended_actions: amend /us/usc/t5/s101: replace section text with the enacted six-department list per PL 19-152 SEC8 (verbatim as enacted), with source-credit and amendment note; do not silently 'correct' the omitted departments.
amend the Code home of PL 12-80 sec.13 (Government Labor Management Act) with the SEC6(2) replacement text, or add a statutory note if that Act is non-positive-law.
record repeals of PL 4-29 (SEC5-1) and PL 12-80 sec.13 (SEC6(1)) as repeal-markings.
capture the Department of Commerce and Labor reorganization/transfers as transfer/statutory notes across affected titles; do not invent Code section text for the organic provisions.
remove Trello link from the note and correct the false 'Repealed or superseded' status.

laws.title: Freedom In Removing Employees and Deregulation Act of 2024
laws.status_basis: Source is HTML viewer debris only: a Google Drive 'Can't download file / owner hasn't given you permission' page. No statutory text was recovered. No lawful codification conclusion can be drawn from the supplied source.

provisions.text_summary: Statutory text unavailable — source is a Google Drive permission-error page.
provisions.evidence: “Sorry, the owner hasn’t given you permission to download this file.”
provisions.notes: Source-limited; text_length 209, html_or_viewer_debris true.

current_implementation.detail: Note claims classification as a statutory note at t5 s101, but no source text exists to justify any placement. Trello link present. Because source is unrecoverable, the note cannot assert any substantive codification result.

recommended_actions: retain only as a source-limited historical note (source unrecoverable/permission-blocked); do NOT assert any Code effect.
remove Trello link.
flag for re-acquisition of the underlying source document before any codification.

laws.title: Fire Department Reform and Nurturing (FDRN)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Organic act establishing the D.C. Fire and Emergency Medical Services Department within DHS. No self-repeal or sunset; not in 'Repealed Laws'. Contains no express amendment to a numbered Code section — it is an uncodified organic/local (District of Columbia) enactment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'FDRN'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Fire Department Reform and Nurturing” or “FDRN”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: General provision (PL 1-1 code applicability).
provisions.evidence: “Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable.”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (Fire Department, ALS, BLS, patient care report, Secretary).
provisions.evidence: “The term “Fire Department” means the District of Columbia Fire and Emergency Medical Services Department...”

provisions.text_summary: Establishes the DC Fire & EMS Department; leadership; mission; firefighters/paramedics; contractors/reporting; resignation notice; home-rule contingency.
provisions.evidence: “There hereby is established the District of Columbia Fire and Emergency Medical Services Department as a Service within the Department of Homeland Security...”
provisions.notes: Local/District organic body; ordinarily not permanent positive-law U.S. Code section text.

current_implementation.detail: Note added at t50 s1. Placement is questionable: this is a District-of-Columbia public-safety organic act, closer to Title 5 (DHS organization) or a DC/Title 40 anchor than Title 50 (War/National Defense). Note reasonably treats it as a statutory note rather than new section text, which is correct for a local organic act, but the anchor is a poor subject-matter fit. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: exclude from permanent positive-law Code section text (local/District organic act); keep a concise statutory note.
relocate the note to a subject-appropriate anchor (DHS/Title 5 or a DC-related title) rather than /us/usc/t50/s1.
remove Trello link.

laws.title: Officer Bill of Rights (OBR)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Free-standing general enactment creating officer procedural rights during internal investigations. No self-repeal/sunset; not in 'Repealed Laws'. Contains no express amendment to a numbered Code section (references 18 U.S.C. 1515(a)(4) only definitionally).

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'OBR'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Officer Bill of Rights” or “OBR””

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (officer, on duty, authorised officer, punitive action, public agency, toll).
provisions.evidence: “The term “officer” means any member of a public agency, or persons defined in 18 U.S. Code. § 1515(a)(4) while on duty”
provisions.notes: 18 U.S.C. 1515(a)(4) cited only for definition; not amended.

provisions.text_summary: Political activity protections for officers.
provisions.evidence: “no officer shall be prohibited from engaging ... in political activity.”

provisions.text_summary: Interrogation/investigation conditions and representation.
provisions.evidence: “the interrogation shall be conducted under the following conditions...”

provisions.text_summary: Lawful exercise of rights; insubordination; administrative appeal; investigation time limits.
provisions.evidence: “No officer shall be subjected to punitive action ... because of the lawful exercise of the rights granted under this act...”

provisions.text_summary: Adverse comments in personnel files; response time; inspection/correction of files.
provisions.evidence: “No officer shall have any comment adverse to his interest entered in his personnel file...”

provisions.text_summary: Applicability, District Court jurisdiction, remedies, civil penalty up to $25,000.
provisions.evidence: “The District Courts shall have initial jurisdiction over any proceeding brought by any officer against any public agency for alleged violations of this act.”

provisions.text_summary: Mutual aid agreements not limited by act.
provisions.evidence: “Nothing in this act shall in any way be construed to limit the use of any public agency ... in the fulfilling of mutual aid agreements...”

current_implementation.detail: Note has has_quoted_content_dump = true (note_char_length 16164 with sha_reference) — an improper full-text dump of the entire act. Placement at /us/usc/t52/s10101/g (voting rights) is a subject-matter mismatch; OBR concerns law-enforcement personnel procedure, not elections. This is a general enactment with no express Code amendment, so a concise statutory note is appropriate, but not a full-text dump.

recommended_actions: remove the full-text quotedContent dump and Trello link from note rp-pl022154-codification.
replace with a concise statutory note; relocate away from t52 (voting) to a subject-appropriate anchor (e.g., Title 5 government-employee procedure or a law-enforcement-personnel provision).
no express Code section requires textual amendment (18 U.S.C. 1515(a)(4) referenced definitionally only).

laws.title: Department of Education Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Operative act: repeals PL 17-129 and abolishes the Department of Education. Not itself repealed. Effect is a repeal/abolition, so it is operative statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: “cited as the “Department of Education Repealment Act”.”

provisions.text_summary: Findings that the Department is a waste of resources.
provisions.evidence: “We as a Congress has found the development of the Departments since it’s foundation stagnant...”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This act shall immediately take effect upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 17-129 in its entirety.
provisions.target: PL 17-129 (Dept of Education organic law, wherever codified)
provisions.exact_change: Repeal PL 17-129 entirely.
provisions.evidence: “Public Law 17-129 shall be repealed in its entirety.”
provisions.notes: If PL 17-129 has Code homes, mark them repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Abolishes the U.S. Department of Education.
provisions.target: Any Title 20 provisions establishing the Department of Education
provisions.exact_change: Department of Education ceases to exist.
provisions.evidence: “The United States Department of Education shall cease to exist.”
provisions.notes: Placement note lists t20 s1 and s2; if those anchor the DoE, they warrant repeal-marking/notes.

current_implementation.detail: Note only. The act repeals PL 17-129 and abolishes the Department of Education; any Code provisions establishing the Department (Title 20) should be repeal-marked, not merely noted. Note declares 'Current or not wholly repealed' — partially inconsistent with an abolition act. Trello link present. Placement spans two anchors.

recommended_actions: repeal-mark any Title 20 (or other) Code provisions establishing the Department of Education, tied to PL 22-155 SEC V.
record repeal of PL 17-129.
keep a concise repeal/abolition statutory note; remove Trello link.

laws.title: Local Law Enforcement Agencies Reform and Nurturing (LLEARN)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Major reorganization act: establishes MPD within DOJ and restructures the U.S. Capitol Police, with dozens of express repeals and full-text substitutions of 2 U.S.C. sections. Not itself repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'LLEARN'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Local Law Enforcement Agencies Reform and Nurturing” or “LLEARN”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately...”

provisions.text_summary: General provision (PL 1-1).
provisions.evidence: “Pursuant to Public Law 1-1...”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions.
provisions.evidence: “The term “Metropolitan Police” means the Metropolitan Police Department, as established in this act.”

provisions.text_summary: Establishes Metropolitan Police Department within DOJ; leadership; structure; powers; PSAs; officers; home-rule contingency.
provisions.evidence: “There hereby is established the Metropolitan Police Department as a bureau within the Department of Justice...”
provisions.notes: Cross-references 5 U.S.C. §§ 1101 et al. for appointments (referential, not amendments).

provisions.text_summary: Repeals numerous prior Public Laws (3-16 remain, 7-45, 16-119, 17-134, 18-143, 11-69) and ~35 sections of Title 2 (Capitol Police) in their entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Strike each listed 2 U.S.C. section in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: “2 U.S.Code §1902 shall be repealed in its entirety.” etc. (enumerated list items 7–37)
provisions.notes: Each is an express repeal of a numbered Code section — requires repeal-marking, not a note.

provisions.text_summary: Strikes 2 U.S.C. 1901a and replaces it (Capitol Police Board establishment, membership, Executive Assistant, documentation).
provisions.exact_change: Replace 1901a with the enacted Establishment/Board-composition/Executive-Assistant/Documentation text.
provisions.evidence: “2 U.S.Code § 1901a shall be repealed in its entirety. 2 U.S.Code §1901a shall now read as; a. Establishment...”
provisions.notes: Determinable replacement text.

provisions.text_summary: Leadership and structure/jurisdiction of Capitol Police (organic).
provisions.evidence: “The principal head of the Capitol Police shall be the Chief of Police...”

provisions.text_summary: Strikes and replaces 2 U.S.C. 1961, 1966, 1966a, 1969, 1974 with new full text (Capitol Police jurisdiction, protection, former-Speaker detail, traffic, emergency deputization).
provisions.exact_change: Replace each listed section with the enacted full text (e.g., 1966 penalty $15,000/8 days; 1969 penalty $10,000/3 days; 1966a former-Speaker 80-day detail).
provisions.evidence: “2 U.S.Code § 1961 shall be stricken in its entirety, and shall now read as follows...”
provisions.notes: Note: SEC7(1) also lists 1966 for outright repeal (item 30) while SEC7(5) rewrites 1966 — internal tension; the later rewrite in SEC7(5) controls the resulting text.

current_implementation.detail: A single boilerplate note at t2 s1. The act performs dozens of express repeals of numbered Title 2 sections and five full-text substitutions (1901a, 1961, 1966, 1966a, 1969, 1974). None were applied as textual amendments/repeal-markings. Massive under-implementation. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: repeal-mark every 2 U.S.C. section enumerated in SEC7(1) (1902–1981 list) tied to PL 22-156.
amend /us/usc/t2/s1901a, s1961, s1966, s1966a, s1969, s1974 with the enacted replacement text (amend-existing-text) with source credits and amendment notes.
reconcile the 1966 repeal-vs-rewrite conflict in favor of the SEC7(5) rewrite; document in an amendment note.
capture MPD organic establishment (SEC6) and Capitol Police leadership (SEC7(3)-(4)) as statutory notes; do not invent Code section text.
remove Trello link and correct the boilerplate.

laws.title: Area Management Update Act (AMUA)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Amends prior Public Law 19-144 (PAMA) sections 6 and 8. Not itself repealed. Amendments target another Public Law, not a numbered U.S. Code section directly.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'AMUA'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Area Management Update Act” or “AMUA”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.

provisions.evidence: “Section 6 of Public Law 19-144, PAMA, shall be amended to state; (11) Organisations inside the Department of Defense with law enforcement powers shall have the authority to close any sidewalk or public road...”

current_implementation.detail: Note only at t18 s1. The act amends PL 19-144 (PAMA) sections 6 and 8 with determinable replacement/insert text. If PAMA is codified, those Code homes require textual amendment. Placement at generic t18 s1 is a poor anchor. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: amend the Code home(s) of PL 19-144 (PAMA) sec.6 and sec.8 with the SEC5(1) and SEC5(2) enacted text, or record as statutory notes if PAMA is non-positive-law.

remove Trello link.

laws.title: Service Members Protection Act (SMPA)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Free-standing general enactment protecting service members' civilian employment during deployment/federalization. No self-repeal/sunset. Cites 10 U.S.C. 12406 definitionally only; no express Code amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'SMPA'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Service Members Protection Act” or “SMPA”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately...”

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (punitive action, deployment, armed forces, military, commanding officer, federalised, service). Cites 10 U.S.C. 12406.
provisions.evidence: ““federalised” ... means when the President calls members or units of the National guard into Federal service, pursuant to 10 U.S. Code §12406”
provisions.notes: 12406 referenced definitionally, not amended. Source skips SEC4.

provisions.text_summary: Substantive protections: military duties take precedence; unlimited leave; no punitive action for missed civilian work; recall of former reservists.
provisions.evidence: “No military personnel may face punitive action for missed work by non-military employment due to their service.”

current_implementation.detail: Note added at t50 s1. General enactment with no express Code amendment, so a statutory note is the correct treatment, but the anchor (Title 50) is a weak fit; Title 10 (Armed Forces) or Title 38 would be more apt. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: keep as a concise statutory note (no express Code amendment).
relocate note to a Title 10 (Armed Forces) anchor rather than t50 s1.
remove Trello link.

laws.title: D.C. Statute of Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Amends PL 11-69 (D.C. criminal code) to add a 'Statute of Limitations' section. Listed in 'Repealed Laws', BUT PL 22-156 SEC7(1) item 6 repeals PL 11-69 in its entirety — so the target of this amendment was subsequently repealed by a later law in this same batch, rendering the amendment inoperative/superseded.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: “cited as the “D.C Statute of Limitations Act”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This act shall immediately take effect upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 11-69 to add a 'Statute of Limitations' section: 75-day limitation for D.C. offenses; commencement rules.
provisions.exact_change: Add a 'Statute of Limitations' section: 75-day limitation period; SoL commences at offense; prosecution requires filing before expiration.
provisions.evidence: “Public Law 11-69 shall be amended to add an additional section titled “Statute of Limitations” that shall henceforth read; i. ... within seventy-five days next after such offense shall have been committed.”
provisions.notes: PL 11-69 was later repealed in entirety by PL 22-156 SEC7(1)(6) — amendment superseded.

current_implementation.detail: Note at t28 s1. This amends PL 11-69, which is repealed in full by PL 22-156 (also in this batch). Any codified effect is superseded/void. Note correctly flags 'Repealed or superseded' status but placement at generic t28 s1 is a poor anchor and the interaction with PL 22-156 is not documented. Local/District subject matter, not general Title 28.

recommended_actions: do NOT apply as a live textual amendment: target PL 11-69 was repealed in entirety by PL 22-156 SEC7(1)(6).
retain only a historical statutory note recording the (superseded) amendment and its repeal by PL 22-156.
remove Trello link.

laws.title: To Amend the U.S. Code
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Express amendment to 18 U.S.C. 205: replaces subsection (a) and inserts a new subsection (j). Not itself repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose — redefine restrictions on practice of law.
provisions.evidence: “To amend the U.S.Code to redefine certain restrictions on the practice of law.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This act shall immediately take effect upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Replaces subsection (a) of 18 U.S.C. 205 with new text (principal officer acting as agent/attorney against U.S.).
provisions.exact_change: Replace 18 U.S.C. 205(a) with the enacted '(a) Whoever, being a principal officer of the United States other than in the proper discharge of his official duties—(1)... (2)... shall be subject to the penalties set forth in section 216 of this title.'
provisions.evidence: “18 U.S.C. §205 shall be amended by replacing subsection (a) with the text; “(a) Whoever, being a principal officer of the United States...””

provisions.text_summary: Inserts a new subsection (j) after subsection (i) of 18 U.S.C. 205 (employee acting as agent/attorney against own Department/Agency).
provisions.exact_change: Insert after (i): '(j) Whoever, being an employee of the United States other than in the proper discharge of his official duties—(1) acts as agent or attorney for prosecuting any claim against any specific government Department or Agency of which they are employed... shall be subject to the penalties set forth in section 216 of this title.'
provisions.evidence: “18 U.S.C. §205 shall be amended by inserting an additional subsection after subsection (i), with the text; “(j) Whoever, being an employee of the United States...””

current_implementation.detail: Note only at t18 s1. This act makes two genuine express amendments to 18 U.S.C. 205 (replace (a); insert (j)) that were NOT applied as textual amendments. Placement should be s205, not s1. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: amend /us/usc/t18/s205: replace subsection (a) and insert subsection (j) per PL 22-160 SEC IV(a)-(b), with source credit and amendment note.
move the anchor from t18 s1 to t18 s205.
remove Trello link.

laws.title: Second Chance Act
laws.status_basis: Source is HTML viewer debris but recovered substantive text for Sections 1-4 (short title, findings, employment standards, sealing petitions). Section 4 and any later sections appear truncated. General enactment; no express amendment to a numbered Code section (Title 18 chapters cited only as exceptions). Partially-current because the recovered portion is coherent but the document is incomplete.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Second Chance Act'; severability; effective date.
provisions.evidence: “This Act may be cited as the “Second Chance Act.””

provisions.text_summary: Findings on employment discrimination based on criminal history.
provisions.evidence: “The Congress finds that employment within the United States should not be discriminant over arrest records or minor criminal convictions...”

provisions.text_summary: Employment standards: bars denial based on arrest records >30 days or convictions >60 days old, with Title 18 chapter exceptions; relief; carve-outs for Presidential appointments, intelligence, security-clearance positions.
provisions.evidence: “Employers, when in consideration of any applicant, shall not deny them on the basis of arrest records dating more than thirty (30) days...”; exceptions list “Title 18, Chapter 20 ... Chapter 115”
provisions.notes: Title 18 chapters cited as exceptions, not amended.

provisions.text_summary: Petition for sealing criminal records in District Court for covered offenses (text truncated in source).
provisions.evidence: “Following a conviction for a covered offense, an eligible person may file a petition for a sealing order concerning the covered offense in the District Court of the United States.”
provisions.notes: Source truncates mid-SEC4; later provisions not recoverable.

current_implementation.detail: Note added at t50 s1 — a clear subject-matter mismatch (Title 50 War/National Defense vs. an employment/criminal-records reentry act). Correct that a statutory note (not new section text) is used, but the anchor is wrong and the source is incomplete. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: keep as a statutory note (general enactment, no express Code amendment); relocate to an employment/criminal-justice anchor (e.g., Title 5 employment or Title 18/28) instead of t50 s1.
flag source as incomplete (HTML debris/truncation); re-acquire full text before finalizing.
remove Trello link.

laws.title: Judicial Fairness Act
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Express amendments to 28 U.S.C. 1253, 1257, 1291; transfer of Chapter 83 authority to the Supreme Court; and amendments to prior Public Laws (17-135, 4-32). Not itself repealed.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: “cited as the “Judicial Fairness Act”.”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: General provisions (PL 1-1).
provisions.evidence: “Pursuant to Public Law 1-1...”

provisions.text_summary: Amends 28 U.S.C. 1253 to a new text (methods of Supreme Court review of district-court cases).
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. 1253 with: 'Cases in the district courts may be reviewed by the Supreme Court by the following methods: a. By writ of certiorari...; b. By certification...; c. Any other method as established by an act of congress or rule of the supreme court.'
provisions.evidence: “28 U.S.Code §1253 shall be amended to read: “Cases in the district courts may be reviewed by the Supreme Court by the following methods...”

provisions.text_summary: Amends 28 U.S.C. 1257 (Supreme Court review of final judgments; mandatory certiorari criteria).
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. 1257 with the enacted appeal/certified-question/certiorari framework and mandatory-grant criteria.
provisions.evidence: “28 U.S.Code §1257 shall be amended to read; a. Final judgments, decrees rendered...”

provisions.text_summary: Transfers all authority of the Court of Appeals (28 U.S.C. Chapter 83) to the Supreme Court.
provisions.exact_change: Transfer all authority vested in the Court of Appeals under 28 U.S.C. Chapter 83 to the Supreme Court.
provisions.evidence: “All authority invested in the Court of Appeals by 28 U.S.Code Chapter 83 shall be transferred to the Supreme Court.”
provisions.notes: Blanket transfer; determinable target chapter, no single strike/insert text — transfer note plus editorial effect.

laws.title: Employee's Bill of Rights
laws.status_basis: Source is HTML viewer debris but recovered substantive text for Sections 1-4 (short title, false terminations, worker's appellate body, unlawful treatment). General enactment on federal employee protections; references Title 5 sec.2302 but does not expressly amend it. Not repealed. Section 5 is cross-referenced by PL 24-173 (‘Section 5 of Public Law 22-163’), confirming the act has ≥5 sections though only 4 recovered here.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Employee's Bill of Rights'; effective date; conflicting-legislation supremacy; definitions (personnel/officer/employee = Executive Branch, excluding Intelligence Community and Military).
provisions.evidence: “This Act may be cited as the “Employee’s Bill of Rights.””; “Any part of any Act ... that conflicts with this Act shall be considered null and void.”

provisions.text_summary: Prohibits personnel actions founded on protected exercises of duty, political retaliation, protected-characteristic (sex, sexual orientation, color, gender, religion), pre-employment activities, or whistleblowing to Congress.
provisions.evidence: “No officer or employee of the United States shall take any personnel action against an employee ... if such personnel action is founded ... in: ...”
provisions.notes: Overlaps subject matter with 5 U.S.C. 2302 but does not textually amend it.

provisions.text_summary: Vests original jurisdiction over federal-employment civil actions in U.S. District Court for D.C.; sealing for Intelligence Community; remedial writs under Title 5 sec.2302.
provisions.evidence: “The United States District Court for the District of Columbia shall have original jurisdiction in all civil actions arising from ... employment ... of federal employees.”
provisions.notes: 5 U.S.C. 2302 referenced, not amended.

provisions.text_summary: Criminal penalty (up to 10 days, termination, or both) for prohibited personnel actions taken with malicious intent.
provisions.evidence: “Any person who performs a personnel action prohibited under Section 2 ... with malicious intent, shall be imprisoned for no more than 10 days, terminated ... or both.”

current_implementation.detail: Note at t5 s101. Statutory-note treatment is appropriate (no express Code amendment), and Title 5 is a reasonable subject match (federal employees), though a Title 5 Chapter 23 anchor (merit systems/2302) would be better than s101. Source is HTML debris; Section 5+ not recovered. Trello link present. Note detected operative_language 'sunset' and 'appropriat' — not confirmed in recovered text; likely from unrecovered sections.

recommended_actions: keep as a statutory note (no express Code amendment); consider anchoring near 5 U.S.C. 2302 (prohibited personnel practices) rather than s101.
flag source as incomplete HTML debris; re-acquire full text (Section 5+ referenced by PL 24-173 but not present).
remove Trello link.

laws.title: Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act (SPAS)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Establishes ODNI clearance-adjudication framework (organic/general) AND expressly amends Sections 213(d) and 214 of Public Law 3-25 (the 'CRACK Act'). Not itself repealed.

provisions.text_summary: ODNI as sole clearance-adjudication authority; counterintelligence investigations; reporting; clearance revocation/suspension; clearance database (with Roblox/Discord IDs); automatic Top Secret positions; issuance tiers; revocation on removal; Gang of Two communications; contractors; designated liaisons; outsourcing.
provisions.evidence: “The Office of the Director of National Intelligence shall be the sole authority of adjudicating security clearances across the Federal government...”
provisions.notes: Organic/general framework; no numbered U.S. Code section amended in SEC3(1)-(12).

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 213(d) of Public Law 3-25 ('CRACK Act') to new text (safeguarding information; DNI classification-markings manual).

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “If any provision of this Act ... is held invalid, the remainder of the Act ... shall not be affected.”

provisions.text_summary: Effective upon enactment.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall become effective upon enactment...”

laws.title: 2nd Amendment Protection Act (2APA)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Express amendment to 18 U.S.C. 930(g)(1) (repeal-and-replace of the 'Federal facility' definition). Listed in 'Repealed Laws' but the source shows no self-repeal and no later law in this batch repeals it; the express Code amendment is operative. laws.title: Affirming Innovation Act (AIA)
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Express amendment to the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 201 et seq.): inserts a new Section 8 (ban on non-compete agreements) after section 7. Not repealed. This is the strongest express-Code-amendment case in the batch (real FLSA codified in Title 29).

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'AIA'.
provisions.evidence: “…“Affirming Innovation Act” or “AIA””

provisions.text_summary: Findings on non-compete agreements harming innovation.
provisions.evidence: “Non-Compete Agreements unjustly limit innovation in the private sector...”

provisions.text_summary: Amends FLSA of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 201 et seq.) by inserting a new SECTION 8 after section 7: definition of non-compete agreement; limitations; non-enforcement of existing agreements; ban on new ones; NDA savings.
provisions.exact_change: Insert new 'SECTION 8. LIMITATIONS ON NON-COMPETE AGREEMENTS' after FLSA section 7 (29 U.S.C. 207), with (a) definition, (b) limitations, (c) non-enforcement/no-new-agreements, (d) NDA savings (referencing 18 U.S.C. 1839 trade-secret definition).
provisions.evidence: “The Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 201 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 7 the following: “SECTION 8. LIMITATIONS ON NON-COMPETE AGREEMENTS...”
provisions.notes: FLSA section 7 = 29 U.S.C. 207; new section codifies within Title 29 Chapter 8. Integrator must determine exact new section number.

provisions.text_summary: Enforcement: equitable relief; repeat-violation imprisonment up to 10 days.
provisions.exact_change: Add enforcement/penalty provisions for violations of the new non-compete section.
provisions.evidence: “Any employer found in violation of The Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ... as amended subsection (a): (1) shall be required to provide equitable relief...; (2) If found in repeat violation shall be subject to imprisonment ... not exceeding 10 days.”

provisions.text_summary: Enactment: amendments take effect 5 days after enactment.
provisions.evidence: “The amendments made by this Act shall take effect 5 days after the date of enactment...”

provisions.text_summary: Applicability to disputes/claims commenced on or after the effective date.
provisions.evidence: “This Act ... shall apply with respect to any dispute or claim for which proceedings commenced on or after the effective date...”

current_implementation.detail: Three notes exist: rp-pl023168-codification (boilerplate at t29 s201, Trello link, correct title match), plus rp-pl023168-208-prior ('Prior Provisions') and rp-pl023168-208-usar ('USAR Amendments') at /us/usc/t29/s208/d. The s208/d notes suggest the integrator attempted placement at 29 U.S.C. 208 — but 29 U.S.C. 208 was repealed in the real Code; the new FLSA section (after section 7 = 29 U.S.C. 207) should be a NEW section, not s208 subsection (d). The express FLSA amendment is recorded only as notes, not as actual new-section text.

recommended_actions: insert the new FLSA non-compete section as actual Code text in Title 29 Chapter 8 after 29 U.S.C. 207 (new-section), not as a note; the integrator must assign the section number.
verify the s208/d placement — 29 U.S.C. 208 is repealed in the real Code; reconsider whether a new section is more appropriate.
add effective-date (SEC4) and applicability (SEC5) notes; source-credit to PL 23-168.
remove Trello link from the codification note.

provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Preamble and findings on employment discrimination against marginalized communities.
provisions.evidence: “Whereas, discrimination based on gender identity, sexual orientation, race ... undermines this principle...”

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a)) to include gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes.
provisions.exact_change: Amend 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add 'gender identity' and 'sexual orientation' as protected classes.
provisions.evidence: “Amend Section 703(a) of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a)) to include gender identity and sexual orientation as protected classes.”
provisions.notes: Real Code target 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2. Note: manifest placement is t29 s201 — WRONG title (should be Title 42).

provisions.text_summary: Expands protections to explicitly include race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, gender identity, sexual orientation across all aspects of employment.
provisions.exact_change: Expand 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2 enumerated protected characteristics to the listed set across hiring, firing, promotion, compensation, and training.
provisions.evidence: “Expand protections against discrimination to explicitly include race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, gender identity, and sexual orientation...”

provisions.text_summary: Enforcement: EEOC authority to investigate expanded-class claims; federal contractor compliance audits.
provisions.evidence: “Provide resources and authority to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) to investigate claims...”

provisions.text_summary: Implementation: Secretary of Labor to promulgate regulations within one year; stakeholder consultation.
provisions.evidence: “Direct the Secretary of Labor to promulgate regulations necessary for the implementation of this Act within one year...”

provisions.text_summary: Conclusion/sense of Congress.
provisions.evidence: “This Act represents a critical step towards achieving equality in employment for all Americans.”

current_implementation.detail: Note rp-pl024178-codification at t29 s201 has has_quoted_content_dump = true (note_char_length 4876, sha_reference) — improper full-text dump. Placement is WRONG title: express amendment targets 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2 (Title 42), not Title 29. The declared codification prose contains false-limitation boilerplate ('No additional direct substitution was made where the enactment used only a blanket clause...') even though SEC6 gives a determinable amendment. The genuine express Title VII amendment was recorded only as a note-with-dump, not applied.

recommended_actions: amend /us/usc/t42/s2000e-2 subsection (a): add 'gender identity' and 'sexual orientation' as protected classes, and expand the enumerated protected characteristics, per PL 24-178 SEC 6(a); add source credit and amendment note.
remove the full-text quotedContent dump and Trello link from note rp-pl024178-codification.
move the anchor from the wrong Title 29 s201 to Title 42 s2000e-2; correct the false source-limitation boilerplate.
add EEOC/Secretary-of-Labor implementation (SEC 6(b), 7) as statutory notes.

laws.title: Fiscal Year 21-2
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Appropriations act for a short fiscal period ('fiscal year ending 30 days following the enactment'). Temporary/expired by its own terms; appropriations do not become permanent Code sections.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Fiscal Year 21-2'; effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “…“Fiscal Year 21-2”.”

provisions.text_summary: Statement of appropriations for the fiscal year ending 30 days after enactment; emergency-requirement availability.
provisions.evidence: “The following sums in this Act are appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, for the fiscal year ending 30 days following the enactment.”
provisions.notes: Time-limited appropriation.

provisions.text_summary: Adjustment to compensation: no COLA under 2 U.S.C. 4501 for members of Congress during FY 21-2.
provisions.exact_change: For FY 21-2 only, no adjustment under section 601(a) of the Legislative Reorganization Act of 1946 (2 U.S.C. 4501).
provisions.evidence: “no adjustment shall be made under section 601(a) of the Legislative Reorganization Act of 1946 (2 U.S.C. 4501) ... during the fiscal year 21-2.”
provisions.notes: Temporary COLA suspension; statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4501, not permanent text.

provisions.text_summary: Enumerated appropriations to Defense, Justice/Law Enforcement, Transportation, State, Homeland Security, Legislative Branch, Miscellaneous, and emergency appropriations.
provisions.evidence: “DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE.—$350,000,000 ... EMERGENCY APPROPRIATIONS. ... $5,925,000,000...”

provisions.text_summary: Monthly reports to Appropriations committees on agency spending.
provisions.evidence: “The office of budget management ... shall submit monthly reports to the House and Senate Appropriations committees...”

current_implementation.detail: Note at t31 s101 preserves as appropriations note. Appropriations should be excluded from permanent Code text; a concise historical note is acceptable but must not become permanent section text. Note declares 'Current or not wholly repealed' though it is a time-limited/expired appropriation. Trello link present. The only quasi-codifiable item (SEC5 temporary COLA suspension re 2 U.S.C. 4501) is not separately noted.

recommended_actions: exclude appropriations provisions from permanent Code (SEC3-4, 6-14); keep at most a concise historical note.
record SEC5 temporary COLA suspension as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4501 (temporary, FY 21-2 only).
correct status to expired/temporary; remove Trello link.

laws.title: Sanction Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: Full text present. Repeals PL 9-55 and lifts/voids sanctions on Iran. Listed in 'Repealed Laws' but source shows no self-repeal; it is itself a repealing act. Operative statutory history.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Sanction Repealment'.
provisions.evidence: “cited as the “Sanction Repealment.””

provisions.text_summary: Purpose — lift economic sanctions in light of expiration of requirements.
provisions.evidence: “It is the purpose of the act to lift economic sanctions in light of the clear expiration of such requirements needed to warrant a sanction.”

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 9-55 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal PL 9-55 entirely.
provisions.evidence: “Be it resolved, that Public Law 9-55 is hereby repealed in its entirety”

provisions.text_summary: Declares all imposed sanctions on the Islamic Republic of Iran null and void; lifted upon enactment.
provisions.evidence: “That all imposed sanctions on the Islamic Republic Iran is hereby declared null and void and shall be lifted upon passage...”

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
provisions.evidence: “This Act shall be severable...”

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately.
provisions.evidence: “This bill shall be effective immediately upon passage...”

current_implementation.detail: Note at t22 s2651 (State/foreign relations) — a reasonable subject anchor. The repeal of PL 9-55 should be recorded as a repeal-marking against its Code home(s), not merely a note. The sanctions-nullification is a concise statutory note. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: repeal-mark the Code home(s) of PL 9-55 (Iran sanctions) per SEC 3(b).
keep the sanctions-nullification (SEC 3(c)) as a concise statutory note at a Title 22 anchor.
remove Trello link.
